assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can install CLI tools if a CLI route is selected.
No Atlas account, organization, project, or cluster exists yet.
No MongoDB deployment product or interaction surface is preselected.

developer_goal: Create a MongoDB Atlas account and reach the earliest meaningful first Atlas data-platform result.

first_success.outcome: A free Atlas cluster is created with access configured and a documented ready or connect signal.
first_success.observable_signal: Either the Atlas UI Overview is available after Security Quickstart, or the Atlas CLI prints the mongosh connection command and cluster-created message.

route_selection.surface: MongoDB Atlas self-service (UI or CLI unresolved)
route_selection.rule: Prefer self-service web when a complete web path exists; do not invent a winner when UI and CLI reach different terminals with consequential setup differences.

candidates.name: Atlas UI Deploy a Free Cluster
candidates.reason: Matches the self-service web preference and documents M0, provider, region, name, database user, and IP access list, but the terminal is Overview rather than a printed connection command.

candidates.name: Atlas CLI atlas setup quickstart
candidates.reason: Official CLI quickstart bundles cluster, sample data, IP allow list, database user, and printed mongosh connect command, but it is a different interaction surface with local install prerequisites.

candidates.name: Local Atlas deployment or driver-specific application path
candidates.reason: Get Started documents local and cloud deployments plus application connect steps as separate products from a single Atlas free-cluster first success.

required_path.action: Register an Atlas account with email, GitHub, or Google and complete the selected identity flow.

required_fields.label: Email Address
required_fields.notes: Required for the email registration tab; OAuth routes use provider identity instead.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy acceptance

required_path.observable_result: The developer can sign in to Atlas.

required_path.action: Use or create the Atlas organization and project that will contain the first cluster.

required_fields.label: Organization and project context
required_fields.notes: Docs say first login may skip steps and auto-create context; exact required fields when manual creation is needed are not fully enumerated here.

required_path.observable_result: A project is available for cluster deployment.

required_path.action: Create and secure the first free cluster using the still-unresolved UI or CLI surface.

required_fields.label: Cluster tier, provider, region, and name, or atlas setup defaults

required_path.observable_result: The free cluster is ready and connection details are available on the selected surface.

prerequisites.description: If the CLI route is selected, Atlas CLI and mongosh must be installed and authenticated.
GitHub signup requires a public email address on the GitHub profile.

external_gates.description: GitHub or Google authorization when those signup methods are chosen.

unavoidable_waits.description: Atlas must finish creating the free cluster before it is ready to use; UI docs state under about 15 seconds.

platform_outcomes.description: Atlas creates the M0 cluster and, on the CLI quickstart, can load sample data and emit a connection string.

excluded.item: Reading MongoDB documentation home and comparing all products
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a product action in the measured path.

excluded.item: Paid Flex or Dedicated cluster checkout
excluded.reason: The free M0 path is documented without requiring paid tiers for the first cluster.

sources.title: MongoDB Documentation
evidence_supported: Cross-product documentation entry without a single universal first surface

sources.title: Create and Configure an Atlas Cluster
evidence_supported: Cluster, sample data, IP allow list, database user

sources.title: Deploy a Free Cluster
evidence_supported: Security Quickstart user and IP steps

sources.title: Create an Atlas Account
evidence_supported: Email form fields and Terms checkbox

uncertainties.question: Should MongoDB's shortest path use Atlas UI, Atlas CLI, local Atlas, or a driver application path?
uncertainties.impact: Each surface changes prerequisites, required actions, first-success signal, and whether local installs count.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Choose one bounded surface and verify its complete current route from a fresh Atlas account against official docs and a product walkthrough.

uncertainties.question: Does a fresh Atlas account always auto-create organization and project, or must the developer create them manually?
uncertainties.impact: Manual organization or project creation would add actions and fields before the first cluster.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough from a brand-new Atlas registration through first project context.
